commit.message:
fix: audit round 3 — security, robustness, accessibility, UX

Server security:
- Fix MAX_PLAYERS race condition with per-room join lock (TOCTOU)
- Add periodic GC for empty game rooms (defense-in-depth)
- Fix rate limiter memory leak in highscores API (periodic eviction)
- Add handler name context to safe() error logging

Server robustness:
- Reject whitespace-only chat messages in validation
- Add null guard for currentPiece in game loop
- Add null safety in host assignment for playerState

Client/UI:
- Fix missed Tailwind v4 class migration (shadow-sm → shadow-xs)
- Add error state for leaderboard fetch failures
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion in chat auto-scroll
- Add initial focus verification fallback in forfeit dialog
- Remove unnecessary empty form action attribute
- Add provider guard to useTheme hook
- Add roundRect browser fallback in mini-board and draw utils

Config:
- Suppress X-Powered-By header (poweredByHeader: false)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: rojasgabriel <88155405+rojasgabriel@users.noreply.github.com>

* Constrain setuptools<80 to restore pkg_resources for datajoint 0.x

setuptools 82 removed pkg_resources from its distribution, breaking
datajoint 0.14.x which imports it directly. Pinning setuptools<80 keeps
pkg_resources available and also prevents the resolver from picking
datajoint>=2.0 (which labdata does not support).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove star prefix from recent subjects, keep color-only distinction

The accent color + bold is sufficient visual distinction; the ★ was
redundant. Updated sidebar legend to read "blue = session in last 2 weeks".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add natural Telegram chat flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(docker): use RUN --mount instead of COPY --from

JuiceFS-backed BuildKit storage doesn't support hash computation
on cross-stage COPY. Use RUN --mount=type=bind to bypass checksum.
Also remove OpenSSL dependency (server uses rustls via reqwest).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add coverage reporting, gosec, and Trivy scanning

- Upload coverage to Codecov on pull requests
- Run gosec static analysis for security issues
- Run Trivy filesystem scan for CRITICAL/HIGH vulnerabilities

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement virtual event enforcement via preToolUse hook

Emulate 4 custom hook events that Copilot CLI doesn't support:
- agentSwitch: validate role name (edit/create + bash), block invalid roles
- memoryWrite: namespace isolation, prevent cross-agent memory corruption
- taskCreate/StatusChange: JSON syntax validation for task-board.json
- chained command defense: per-segment analysis prevents whitelist bypass

Also fixes:
- Reviewer can now switch roles (virtual event bypasses file restriction)
- grep pipefail crash on no-match (added || true)
- Chained command bypass (echo > .agents/... && rm src/) now caught

Test results: 41/41 (5 suite + 15 false-positive + 21 virtual-event)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
:construction_worker: enable auto-merge on update-licenses PRs (#1114)

PRs created by the `update-licenses` workflow were not auto-merging
after CI passed, requiring manual intervention.

## Changes

- **`update-licenses.yml`**
  - Add `id: cpr` to `Create Pull Request` step to expose its outputs
- Add `Enable Auto Merge` step using
`peter-evans/enable-pull-request-automerge@v3`, triggered only on
`created` (skips on `updated`), squash merge strategy

```yaml
- name: Create Pull Request
  id: cpr
  uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v8
  ...

- name: Enable Auto Merge
  if: steps.cpr.outputs.pull-request-operation == 'created'
  uses: peter-evans/enable-pull-request-automerge@v3
  with:
    token: ${{ secrets.PAT }}
    pull-request-number: ${{ steps.cpr.outputs.pull-request-number }}
    merge-method: squash
```

> **Prerequisite:** Repository Settings → General → **Allow auto-merge**
must be enabled.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: SorrowBlue <25735189+SorrowBlue@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: reconcile full Deployment spec instead of selected fields

Compare existing vs desired Spec before updating. Replace the entire
Spec on update to avoid missing fields like Strategy or
MinReadySeconds.

commit.message:
security: add trap cleanup for SFTP temp files

Register EXIT trap to remove temporary SSH key file and known_hosts
file on script exit. Prevents SSH private key material from being
left on disk in shared /tmp directory after failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#144): instant scroll + near-bottom guard, fixes snap regression and TC5 (#146)

- Replace animateScrollToItem with instant scrollToItem to eliminate
  animation/gesture mutex conflict causing snap-back during streaming
- Add near-bottom guard (lastVisible >= size-2): if user has scrolled up
  to read history, new messages no longer yank them back to the bottom
  (the scroll-to-bottom button handles that use case)
- Fixes TC5: 'send message while scrolled up → should NOT auto-scroll'

commit.message:
Fix git checkout failure during rework: reset before branch switch

SyncWithMainAsync does git checkout main, which fails if the
workspace has uncommitted changes from a prior failed rework.
Adding git reset --hard HEAD before checkout ensures clean state.

  filters blank entries to handle double-comma input (e.g. field1,,field2)

commit.message:
chore: add CODE_REVIEW_TASKS_V3.md to gitignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(#139): synchronise table column widths using SubcomposeLayout (#145)

Replace the Row/Column + defaultMinSize approach with a three-pass
SubcomposeLayout that guarantees every cell in the same column has
an identical pixel width:

  Pass 1 ("w-r-c"): measure each cell unconstrained → colWidths[]
  Pass 2 ("h-r-c"): measure at fixed colWidth → rowHeights[]
  Pass 3 ("l-r-c"): measure at exact (colWidth × rowHeight) → place

weight() is still avoided (crashes in horizontalScroll's unbounded
constraints). fillMaxHeight() is a no-op during passes 1 & 2
(unbounded height) and fills to the fixed rowHeight in pass 3,
keeping all cells in a row the same height — replacing the previous
IntrinsicSize.Min Row wrapper.

Removes the now-unused defaultMinSize import.
Preview MarkdownTableAlignmentPreview (#130) still compiles.

Co-authored-by: NickMonrad <nick@lab3.com.au>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Normalize LLM grammar gap IDs to catalog structure IDs

Root cause: post-session consolidation LLM generates free-form structure
IDs ('article_usage', 'verb_conjugation', 'adjective_agreement') that
don't match our curated catalog IDs ('articles_definis_indefinis',
'present_tense', 'adjectifs_accord'). This caused the roadmap to show
'Conversation libre' for most lessons (no theme matches) and the
progress overview to miss most learned structures.

Fix: Added normalize_gap_id() mapping with ~50 known LLM→catalog
mappings. Applied normalization in:
- Planner: review structure selection + introducible computation
- Roadmap: mastery overview + structures in rotation
- Catalog API: structure status enrichment

Result: Roadmap now shows themed lessons (Cuisine, Routine quotidienne)
with matching phrase categories instead of generic conversation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- resume_task: full lifecycle (13 tests) — success, failure, retry,

Total: 1332 tests, 239 lines missed (96% overall coverage).

commit.message:
feat: Tier 2 detail pages — 8 more licensing plans

New detail pages:
- Office 365 E1 () — budget enterprise, E1 vs E3 vs M365 E3
- Office 365 E3 () — O365 vs M365 comparison, 'car without insurance' analogy
- Office 365 E5 () — niche use case, O365 E5 vs M365 E5
- Business Basic () — Basic vs Standard vs Premium progression
- Frontline F1 & F3 (/) — combined page, shift worker focus
- Copilot Chat (free) — what changed April 2026, Chat vs paid Copilot
- Security Copilot (/SCU) — pay-per-use model explained
- Power Automate per User () — attended vs unattended RPA

Total: 19 detail pages live, 20 plans with View Details buttons.

commit.message:
feat: MCP client runtime — JSON-RPC 2.0, server manager, registry client

- Add crates/caduceus-mcp to workspace
- types.rs: McpServerConfig, McpTransport (stdio/http), McpToolDef,
  McpResource, McpRegistryEntry, ServerStatus, McpPackage, PackageRuntime
- error.rs: McpError with IO/HTTP/JsonRpc/Config variants
- client.rs: McpClient with connect (stdio process spawn + HTTP),
  JSON-RPC 2.0 send/receive, list_tools, call_tool, list_resources,
  read_resource, MCP initialize handshake
- manager.rs: McpServerManager — add/remove servers, start_all,
  all_tools (deduplicated), call_tool routing, health_check, shutdown_all
- registry.rs: McpRegistryClient — search, get_server, install
  (generates McpServerConfig from registry entry packages)
- 24 tests covering: JSON-RPC serialization, response parsing, tool def
  parsing, config loading, manager add/remove/routing, tool aggregation,
  registry entry parsing, install for npm/python/docker runtimes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE_FILE exists (Mnemo.exe + MnemoSetup.exe).
- Decode PFX from WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE_PFX_B64 in release + windows-build workflows.

commit.message:
feat: Enhanced study guides batch 3 — AZ-305, AZ-400, SC-200, PL-300

AZ-305 (Azure Architect Expert):
- 49 objectives across 4 domains with deep links
- Identity/governance, data storage, business continuity, infrastructure
- Well-Architected Framework + Cloud Adoption Framework references

AZ-400 (DevOps Expert):
- 86 objectives across 5 domains — 50-55% on build/release pipelines
- GitHub Actions, Azure Pipelines, IaC, deployment strategies
- GitHub Advanced Security, Defender for DevOps, workload identity federation

SC-200 (Security Operations Analyst):
- 54 objectives across 3 domains with deep links
- Defender XDR, Sentinel SIEM, incident response, KQL threat hunting
- Updated April 16, 2026 with major restructuring + Copilot embedded

PL-300 (Power BI Data Analyst):
- 78 objectives across 4 domains with deep links
- Power Query, DAX, data modelling, visualisation, Copilot features
- Star schema design, RLS, calculation groups, visual calculations

Total enhanced guides: 13 of 52

commit.message:
Add main entry, App router, test suite, and vitest config

- Add src/main.tsx entry point with StrictMode root mounting
- Add src/index.css global reset and base styles
- Add src/app/App.tsx with BrowserRouter and three routes
- Add vitest.config.ts with node environment targeting src/test/**
- Add src/test/spec/parser.test.ts (12 tests)
- Add src/test/pipeline/parseTypeScript.test.ts (4 tests)
- Add src/test/pipeline/validatePurity.test.ts (7 tests)
- Add src/test/pipeline/buildIR.test.ts (3 tests)

Fix bugs uncovered by tests:
- parser.ts: parseLiteral now handles negative number literals (e.g. -1)
- parseTypeScript.ts: check for import/class constructs before running
  diagnostics so they surface as UnsupportedConstructError

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: xanticious <16355332+xanticious@users.noreply.github.com>

Support composite tag rules for record tagging (#189)

* Initial plan

* feat: support composite tag rules for record tagging

- Add ConditionsJson column to sobs_tag_rules table (schema + migration)
- Update _load_tag_rules to parse ConditionsJson into 'conditions' list
- Refactor _match_tag_rule to delegate to new _match_single_condition helper
- Composite rules (non-empty conditions list) require ALL conditions to match
- Simple (legacy) rules continue to use single match_field/operator/value
- Update create_tag_rule route to accept condition_field[]/operator[]/value[]/attr_key[] arrays
- Store composite conditions in ConditionsJson; primary condition in legacy columns for compat
- Update delete_tag_rule to include ConditionsJson in soft-delete row
- Update auto_tag_rules to populate ConditionsJson for auto-created rules
- Update settings_tags.html: dynamic 'Add Condition' UI with composite badge
- Update rules table to show all conditions with AND labels and composite badge
- Update 'How It Works' docs to describe composite rules
- Add tests: composite all match, partial match, attribute condition, wrong record type

repository.name: sobs
repository.description: Simple Observe Stack (sobs) is a lightweight application RUM, telemetry, logs, errors and AI focused container for application insights. Primarily focused on single user, developers, vibe coders and alike for minimal infrastructure and transparency, based on open telemetry standards.

commit.message:
chore: remove unsupported custom events from Copilot hooks config

Copilot CLI v1.0.24 only supports 6 built-in events:
sessionStart, sessionEnd, userPromptSubmitted, preToolUse,
postToolUse, errorOccurred.

Removed 6 custom events that were silently ignored:
agentSwitch, taskCreate, taskStatusChange, memoryWrite,
compaction, goalVerified.

Claude Code hooks.json retained (may support custom events).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
